contentBlocks.original:
श्रीरामसौमित्रिजटायुवेद षडाननादित्य कुजार्चिताय ।
श्रीनीलकण्ठाय दयामयाय श्रीवैद्यनाथाय नमःशिवाय ॥ 1॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīrāmasaumitrijaṭāyuvēda ṣaḍānanāditya kujārchitāya ।
śrīnīlakaṇṭhāya dayāmayāya śrīvaidyanāthāya namaḥśivāya ॥ 1॥
contentBlocks.translation: Worshipped by Sri Rama, Lakshmana, Jatayu, the six-faced Kartikeya, the sun and Mars — O blue-throated, compassionate one — salutations to Sri Vaidyanatha, the Lord as Shiva.

contentBlocks.original:
गङ्गाप्रवाहेन्दु जटाधराय त्रिलोचनाय स्मर कालहन्त्रे ।
समस्त देवैरभिपूजिताय श्रीवैद्यनाथाय नमः शिवाय ॥ 2॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gaṅgāpravāhēndu jaṭādharāya trilōchanāya smara kālahantrē ।
samasta dēvairabhipūjitāya śrīvaidyanāthāya namaḥ śivāya ॥ 2॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who bears the flowing Ganga and the moon in his matted locks, three-eyed, destroyer of Kama and Kala — worshipped by all the gods — salutations to Sri Vaidyanatha, the Lord as Shiva.

contentBlocks.original:
भक्तःप्रियाय त्रिपुरान्तकाय पिनाकिने दुष्टहराय नित्यम् ।
प्रत्यक्षलीलाय मनुष्यलोके श्रीवैद्यनाथाय नमः शिवाय ॥ 3॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaktaḥpriyāya tripurāntakāya pinākinē duṣṭaharāya nityam ।
pratyakṣalīlāya manuṣyalōkē śrīvaidyanāthāya namaḥ śivāya ॥ 3॥
contentBlocks.translation: Dear to his devotees, destroyer of Tripura, bearer of the Pinaka bow, eternal destroyer of the wicked — who performs visible miracles in the human world — salutations to Sri Vaidyanatha, the Lord as Shiva.

contentBlocks.original:
प्रभूतवातादि समस्तरोग प्रनाशकर्त्रे मुनिवन्दिताय ।
प्रभाकरेन्द्वग्नि विलोचनाय श्रीवैद्यनाथाय नमः शिवाय ॥ 4॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prabhūtavātādi samastarōga pranāśakartrē munivanditāya ।
prabhākarēndvagni vilōchanāya śrīvaidyanāthāya namaḥ śivāya ॥ 4॥
contentBlocks.translation: Destroyer of all ailments such as excess of wind (vata) and other disorders, worshipped by sages — whose three eyes are the sun, moon and fire — salutations to Sri Vaidyanatha, the Lord as Shiva.

contentBlocks.original:
वाक् श्रोत्र नेत्राङ्घ्रि विहीनजन्तोः वाक्श्रोत्रनेत्राङ्घ्रिसुखप्रदाय ।
कुष्ठादिसर्वोन्नतरोगहन्त्रे श्रीवैद्यनाथाय नमः शिवाय ॥ 5॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vāk śrōtra nētrāṅghri vihīnajantōḥ vākśrōtranētrāṅghrisukhapradāya ।
kuṣṭhādisarvōnnatarōgahantrē śrīvaidyanāthāya namaḥ śivāya ॥ 5॥
contentBlocks.translation: Who grants speech, hearing, sight, and the ability to walk to creatures born bereft of them — destroyer of all grievous diseases such as leprosy — salutations to Sri Vaidyanatha, the Lord as Shiva.

contentBlocks.original:
वेदान्तवेद्याय जगन्मयाय योगीश्वरद्येय पदाम्बुजाय ।
त्रिमूर्तिरूपाय सहस्रनाम्ने श्रीवैद्यनाथाय नमः शिवाय ॥ 6॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vēdāntavēdyāya jaganmayāya yōgīśvaradyēya padāmbujāya ।
trimūrtirūpāya sahasranāmnē śrīvaidyanāthāya namaḥ śivāya ॥ 6॥
contentBlocks.translation: Known through Vedanta, all-pervading as the universe itself, whose lotus feet are meditated upon by great yogis — of the form of the Holy Trinity, bearing a thousand names — salutations to Sri Vaidyanatha, the Lord as Shiva.

contentBlocks.original:
स्वतीर्थमृद्भस्मभृताङ्गभाजां पिशाचदुःखार्तिभयापहाय ।
आत्मस्वरूपाय शरीरभाजां श्रीवैद्यनाथाय नमः शिवाय ॥ 7॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svatīrthamṛdbhasmabhṛtāṅgabhājāṃ piśāchaduḥkhārtibhayāpahāya ।
ātmasvarūpāya śarīrabhājāṃ śrīvaidyanāthāya namaḥ śivāya ॥ 7॥
contentBlocks.translation: To those who smear their limbs with sacred ash and clay from his holy tirtha — destroyer of the afflictions and fears caused by evil spirits, the very Self dwelling in all bodies — salutations to Sri Vaidyanatha, the Lord as Shiva.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीनीलकण्ठाय वृषध्वजाय स्रक्गन्ध भस्माद्यभिशोभिताय ।
सुपुत्रदारादि सुभाग्यदाय श्रीवैद्यनाथाय नमः शिवाय ॥ 8॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīnīlakaṇṭhāya vṛṣadhvajāya srakgandha bhasmādyabhiśōbhitāya ।
suputradārādi subhāgyadāya śrīvaidyanāthāya namaḥ śivāya ॥ 8॥
contentBlocks.translation: O blue-throated one bearing the bull-banner, adorned with garlands, sandal paste, sacred ash and other ornaments — bestower of the blessings of noble children, a devoted spouse, and all-round fortune — salutations to Sri Vaidyanatha, the Lord as Shiva.

contentBlocks.original:
बालाम्बिकेश वैद्येश भवरोग हरेति च ।
जपेन्नामत्रयं नित्यं महारोगनिवारणम् ॥ 9॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bālāmbikēśa vaidyēśa bhavarōga harēti cha ।
japēnnāmatrayaṃ nityaṃ mahārōganivāraṇam ॥ 9॥
contentBlocks.translation: O consort of Balaambika, O Vaidyesha, O remover of the disease of worldly existence — one who daily chants these three names attains relief from all great disease. (Phalashruti)
